howItWorks.intro: This stock profit calculator uses standard formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Profit = (Sell Price - Buy Price) × Shares - Commissions
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates. Consult a professional for critical decisions.

faq.question: How is stock profit calculated?
faq.answer: Profit = (Sell Price × Shares - Commission) - (Buy Price × Shares + Commission). This is your gross profit before taxes.

faq.question: What about taxes on stock profits?
faq.answer: Short-term gains (held under 1 year) are taxed as ordinary income. Long-term gains (over 1 year) get preferential rates of 0%, 15%, or 20%.

faq.question: What about dividends?
faq.answer: This calculator covers trading profit only. Dividends received during your holding period would add to your total return.

faq.question: Are commissions still a factor?
faq.answer: Most major brokers (Fidelity, Schwab, Robinhood) now offer $0 commission stock trades. Options and some other products may still have fees.

howWeCalculate.intro: This stock profit calculator uses established formulas and current data to provide accurate estimates.

howWeCalculate.disclaimer: These calculations are estimates based on standard formulas. For critical decisions, always consult a qualified professional.

whenToUse.intro: This stock profit calculator is designed for anyone who needs quick, reliable calculations without complex spreadsheets or professional consultations.
scenarios: When planning major financial decisions like buying a home, investing, or taking on debt
When comparing different financial products or scenarios side by side
When building a budget or projecting future savings and expenses
When you want to verify a quote or estimate from a financial advisor or lender
When teaching or learning about personal finance and compound growth
